help.stdoutText:
Agent-first issue tracker (SQLite + JSONL)

Usage: br [OPTIONS] <COMMAND>

Commands:
  agents       Manage AGENTS.md workflow instructions
  audit        Record and label agent interactions (append-only JSONL)
  blocked      List blocked issues
  changelog    Generate changelog from closed issues
  close        Close an issue
  comments     Manage comments
  completions  Generate shell completions
  config       Configuration management
  count        Count issues with optional grouping
  create       Create a new issue
  defer        Defer issues (schedule for later)
  delete       Delete an issue (creates tombstone)
  dep          Manage dependencies
  doctor       Run diagnostics and optionally repair issues
  epic         Epic management commands
  graph        Visualize dependency graph
  history      Manage local history backups
  info         Show diagnostic metadata about the workspace
  init         Initialize a beads workspace
  label        Manage labels
  lint         Check issues for missing template sections
  list         List issues
  orphans      List orphan issues (referenced in commits but open)
  q            Quick capture (create issue, print ID only)
  query        Manage saved queries
  ready        List ready issues (open, unblocked, not deferred)
  reopen       Reopen an issue
  schema       Emit JSON Schemas and per-command output envelope shapes (for agent/tooling integration)
  search       Search issues
  show         Show issue details
  stale        List stale issues
  stats        Show project statistics
  status       Alias for stats
  sync         Sync database with JSONL file (export or import)
  undefer      Undefer issues (make ready again)
  update       Update an issue
  upgrade      Upgrade br to the latest version
  version      Show version information
  where        Show the active .beads directory
  help         Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)

Options:
      --db <DB>                      Database path (auto-discover .beads/*.db if not set)
      --actor <ACTOR>                Actor name for audit trail
      --json                         Output as JSON
      --no-daemon                    Force direct mode (no daemon) - effectively no-op in br v1
      --no-auto-flush                Skip auto JSONL export
      --no-auto-import               Skip auto import check
      --allow-stale                  Allow stale DB (bypass freshness check warning)
      --lock-timeout <LOCK_TIMEOUT>  `SQLite` busy/write-lock timeout in ms
      --no-db                        JSONL-only mode (no DB connection)
  -v, --verbose...                   Increase logging verbosity (-v, -vv)
  -q, --quiet                        Quiet mode (no output except errors)
      --no-color                     Disable colored output
  -h, --help                         Print help
  -V, --version                      Print version

ready_json.stdoutText:
[
  {
    "created_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:38.439615181Z",
    "created_by": "ubuntu",
    "description": "Train/adapt a quantized EnCodec/HuBERT variant producing joint [s_n, a_n] latent vectors: s_n (semantic latent, text-decodable) and a_n (acoustic residual, prosody/timbre). Open research questions: Can single encoder produce BOTH text-decodable semantics AND high-fidelity acoustics? Does joint objective degrade audio vs dedicated Opus? Does it generalize beyond English? Kill criteria apply (see epic description).",
    "id": "nexusaudio-9ga.1",
    "issue_type": "task",
    "priority": 4,
    "status": "open",
    "title": "Joint neural encoder training/adaptation",
    "updated_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:38.439615181Z"
  }
]

list_json.stdoutText:
{
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "nexusaudio-9ga.3",
      "title": "Neural token decoder and latent-space reconstruction",
      "description": "Lightweight token decoder (~500KB, single linear layer + CTC) mapping s_n to text tokens in < 1ms. Latent-space reconstruction R_phi for enhanced cross-representation repair: A_hat(t) = R_phi(s_n, a_context) where a_context is acoustic residual from surrounding frames. Hypothesized POLQA > 3.8 for gaps < 300ms (vs classic TTS path POLQA > 3.2). This improvement comes from operating in learned representation space rather than lossy text->TTS roundtrip.",
      "status": "open",
      "priority": 4,
      "issue_type": "task",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:38.680251896Z",
      "created_by": "ubuntu",
      "updated_at": "2026-03-21T23:50:31.738310998Z",
      "source_repo": ".",
      "compaction_level": 0,
      "original_size": 0,
      "labels": [
        "decoder",
        "r3",
        "research"
      ],
      "dependency_count": 2,
      "dependent_count": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "nexusaudio-9ga.2",
      "title": "WebGPU neural encoder inference (< 2ms per 10ms frame)",
      "description": "WebGPU inference for the neural encoder at < 2ms per 10ms audio frame on mid-range GPU. WASM SIMD fallback at < 5ms per frame. The encoder replaces both Opus codec and STT pipeline simultaneously. Auto-detected via capability probe (WebGPU availability, measured frame budget, device memory, hardwareConcurrency, thermal/perf history). ~20MB model loaded on-demand. Must produce compatible room artifacts with classic path.",
      "status": "open",
      "priority": 4,
      "issue_type": "task",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:38.564510956Z",
      "created_by": "ubuntu",
      "updated_at": "2026-03-21T23:50:31.687966744Z",

        "inference",
        "r3",
        "research"
      ],
      "dependency_count": 2,
      "dependent_count": 0
    },
    {
      "id": "nexusaudio-9ga.1",
      "title": "Joint neural encoder training/adaptation",
      "description": "Train/adapt a quantized EnCodec/HuBERT variant producing joint [s_n, a_n] latent vectors: s_n (semantic latent, text-decodable) and a_n (acoustic residual, prosody/timbre). Open research questions: Can single encoder produce BOTH text-decodable semantics AND high-fidelity acoustics? Does joint objective degrade audio vs dedicated Opus? Does it generalize beyond English? Kill criteria apply (see epic description).",
      "status": "open",
      "priority": 4,
      "issue_type": "task",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:38.439615181Z",
      "created_by": "ubuntu",
      "updated_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:38.439615181Z",
      "source_repo": ".",
      "compaction_level": 0,
      "original_size": 0,
      "labels": [
        "encoder",
        "r3",
        "research"
      ],
      "dependency_count": 1,
      "dependent_count": 2
    },
    {
      "id": "nexusaudio-9ga",
      "title": "R3: Neural Encoder Path",
      "description": "Joint neural encoder producing both semantic latent vector s_n (text-decodable) and acoustic residual a_n (prosody/timbre) from a single forward pass. This is exploratory — the classic Opus+STT path carries the full architectural weight. If Phase R3 fails entirely, no production feature is degraded. KILL CRITERIA (any one triggers deprioritization): WER > 30% worse than server Whisper; POLQA > 0.3 MOS worse than Opus at matched bitrate; inference > 4ms per 10ms frame on 25th-percentile WebGPU; joint training fails to converge; approach doesn't generalize beyond English. HARD DECISION GATE: 18 months of active research.",
      "status": "open",
      "priority": 4,
      "issue_type": "epic",
      "created_at": "2026-03-21T22:33:50.632956850Z",
      "created_by": "ubuntu",
      "updated_at": "2026-03-21T22:44:56.290824535Z",

      "original_size": 0,
      "labels": [
        "neural",
        "r3",
        "research"
      ],
      "dependency_count": 1,
      "dependent_count": 3
    }
  ],

}
